relevant.title: videocore stops dispatching all events permanently after a client disconnects mid-playback (orphaned currentState.ClientId)
relevant.why: Our HEAD already contains an explicit fix referencing this exact issue number: internal/videocore/videocore.go:932-944 has a `// #814:` comment describing the orphaned-ClientId gate and a rebind path (falls through on video-loaded, or calls vc.rebindClient when the bound client is no longer connected). Given this touches the exact global/per-session event-plane gating pattern this audit profile cares about, worth confirming it was live-tested for our nakama/multi-user setup.

relevant.title: Browser direct play only works for H.264+AAC files (malformed mimeCodec strings)
relevant.why: Confirmed present at internal/mediastream/videofile/info.go:398 - `ret += fmt.Sprintf(".L%02X.BO", stream.Level)` for HEVC uses hex level + literal letter O instead of digit 0 (should be decimal + `.B0`), so HEVC never satisfies the browser's canPlayType() check and always falls back to transcoding; AV1 level and Opus/VP9 mappings have the same class of bug per the report. Directly affects local-library browser playback (part of our deployment profile) on any non-H.264 file.

relevant.title: Subtitle tracks fail with "no file has been loaded" when reopening an episode in the browser player
relevant.why: Confirmed `useRequestMediastreamMediaContainer` in seanime-web/src/api/hooks/mediastream.hooks.ts:36-41 is a tanstack query wrapping `RequestMediastreamMediaContainer` (a POST with a server-side side effect registering the active file) with no gcTime override, matching the reported race where a cached response lets the player mount and fetch subs before the file is re-registered. Reproduces on reopening a recently-watched local episode or after a server restart with an already-open SPA tab.

relevant.title: MpvCore player blacks don't look right (letterbox blacks visibly lighter than player background)
relevant.why: Same rendering subsystem (mpv-prism/Electron MpvCore) as the black-frame-flashing bug we just fixed via the WGL backend default (v3.9.4) per memory denshi-gpu-flags-black-frames.md, but this is a distinct symptom (color/gamma mismatch, not flashing) - unclear whether the WGL switch incidentally fixed it too; would need a live check on the Windows build.

relevant.title: Use Nakama Host's anime library for the Schedule Tab when connected as a peer
relevant.why: Grep for "Schedule" in internal/nakama turns up nothing, consistent with the report that the Schedule tab always reads the peer's own local library rather than the host's shared library when connected via Nakama, which is a core usage mode in our deployment profile (nakama watch rooms).

relevant.title: Auto Downloader should make separate RSS request per anime (Nyaa 75-item feed cap causes missed episodes on non-uptime PCs)
relevant.why: This is the built-in Go autodownloader (internal/library/autodownloader), a separate code path from the external Nyaa/AIOStreams addon our multi-season-pack fix touched (per memory multiseason-pack-and-nyaa-addon-deploy.md); the described single-feed-with-75-item-cap design is a plausible real limitation for any Denshi/desktop user who doesn't keep the app running 24/7.

relevant.title: Skip the file-selection confirmation screen when a torrent/batch has only one file
relevant.why: Plausible UX friction in the file-selection step shared by torrentstream/debridstream flows that our deployment relies on (TorBox debrid streaming), but a 1-minute grep didn't locate the exact single-file gating logic to confirm status.

relevant.title: Automatic episode source doesn't fall back to torrent stream when local library is missing the next episode
relevant.area: playback source auto-selection (local library + torrent/debrid fallback)
relevant.why: Touches the auto-source-selection logic relevant to our local-library-plus-debrid-streaming setup, but could not locate the specific "automatic source" selection code within the 1-minute budget to confirm whether this fallback gap still exists at HEAD.

relevant.title: Add network interface binding for built-in torrent streaming client (VPN leak protection)
relevant.why: Feature gap rather than a bug; relevant since our profile includes a local library with magnet-to-library torrent downloads, but it's a security hardening request, not a functional break, and not something we've specifically encoded a fix for.

relevant.title: Improve skip op/ed to detect more chapter title variations (Opening/Ending/Intro/Credits, case-insensitive)
relevant.why: Straightforward matching-logic gap (only literal "OP"/"ED" chapter names recognized) in a feature applicable to our local-library playback path; low severity, cosmetic/functional convenience only.

relevant.title: feature request: add Usenet support for TorBox Debrid
relevant.why: We use TorBox as primary debrid provider (internal/debrid/torbox/torbox.go); grep confirms no usenet handling exists anywhere in internal/, so the gap applies equally to our fork. Purely additive feature, not a regression.

relevant.title: feature request: Support for .strm files
relevant.why: Request explicitly cites TorBox debrid workflows (storageless library via remote-URL text files), overlapping our debrid-streaming + magnet-to-library feature area; grep found no .strm handling in internal/library, so the gap exists in our fork too. Different mechanism than our directstream architecture, so not a duplicate of existing work.

relevant.title: feature request: Add volume boost in the new built in media player
relevant.area: videocore / browser player (also relevant to Denshi MpvCore)
relevant.why: Tagged 'videocore' upstream, which we ship and use as our primary browser-side player (Denshi/MpvCore also plays low-volume BDRip audio per our own memory notes on audio tracks); grep for gain-node/volume-boost in seanime-web found nothing, so cap is still 100% in our fork.

relevant.title: bug: Openings/Endings with variants not recognising properly (AniDB OP1a/OP1b)
relevant.area: library scanner / hydrator metadata (AniDB OP/ED numbering)
relevant.why: Scanner/hydrator files that would own this logic (internal/library/scanner/hydrator.go) exist unchanged in this area as far as a quick check shows; low severity, cosmetic metadata-sort issue only affecting users who separately track OP/ED files.

relevant.title: feature request: display tags on library items and queries
relevant.why: Touches our used library-entry query/filter path (schema + handlers + frontend filtering.ts) but a quick grep did not conclusively show whether tag data is already exposed; requester has a working personal-fork patch upstream hasn't merged.

relevant.title: request: Sync Settings & Extensions (Cross-Device)
relevant.why: We do use local settings + extensions (AIOStreams/Nyaa addons) that would benefit from cross-device sync in a multi-client (web/Denshi/Tenji) setup, but this is a broad, already low-prio upstream feature request with no fork-specific angle.

notes: Fetched 50 open issues (page 1 of 2, gh api search/issues, first try succeeded, no retry needed). Most of the 50 are pure feature requests for areas outside our deployment profile (manga volume support, Discord custom presence, plugin UI components, Android data-dir, MPRIS on desktop Linux, Chromecast/DLNA, Windows ARM64 builds, reverse-proxy base-path, config-file editing UI, tray icon reordering, carousel/scroll UX, planning-list sort, playlist episode cap, online-streaming-provider quality/persistence) and were excluded per task instructions (not touching code paths central to our multi-user Pi/debrid/Denshi/Tenji/nakama profile, or genuinely not applicable e.g. Android). #814 is the standout: our fork already carries an explicit fix citing the issue number verbatim in code, so no action needed there beyond confirming it's been live-validated for the multi-user/nakama scenarios this audit profile emphasizes. #866 and #868 are the two highest-confidence *unaddressed* bugs, both have precise upstream-reported code locations that I independently confirmed still match our HEAD verbatim. Two items (823, 820) were left as 'unknown' rather than chased further, per the 1-minute-check guidance, since confirming would need reading torrentstream/library-explorer selection logic in more depth than the budget allowed. Two nakama/local-library issues in the excluded set (810 manga-nakama-sync, 806 default-provider-persistence) were judged low-value duplicates of themes already covered elsewhere and left out to keep the list under budget.

Page 2 of upstream open issues (14 total, single search/issues call, no retry needed). Excluded as not touching our deployment profile: #502 (manga auto-download — manga not part of our stated profile), #467 (title-language toggle without AniList login — we require/use AniList), #456 (offline search/stats — we run networked multi-user online mode), #418 (manga reading-mode auto-switch, upstream-labeled 'status: not planned'), #353 (extension marketplace sorting UI — cosmetic, extensions we use aren't affected functionally), #344 (manga 'Refresh Sources' bulk-discovery — manga-specific, not in our profile). None of the 14 issues on this page were bug reports mapping to previously-known fork issues (nakama/mediacore/directstream/admin-claim/etc.) — this page skewed toward feature requests plus one minor metadata bug (#476), and no items recur from fable-audit.md's tracked keyword set. All 'likelyInFork' calls for feature requests were interpreted as 'does this gap still exist unaddressed in our fork' (grep-verified absence of volume-boost/gain-node, .strm, and usenet handling in internal/ and seanime-web/src) rather than a code-defect classification, since these are new-feature asks, not regressions.

confirmed.title: Size tie-break assumes 'bigger = higher bitrate', but batches make that false
confirmed.detail: bc317d74 adds `if a.torrent.Size != b.torrent.Size { return cmp.Compare(b.torrent.Size, a.torrent.Size) }` as a tiebreak in both sortCandidates and smartCachedPrioritization, justified as 'higher bitrate ≈ better quality at the same resolution'. That assumption breaks when a multi-episode batch ties a single episode in the same audio/cache/format tier (BatchPreference defaults to neutral, contributing 0 to score, so no separation happens beforehand) — the batch's Size reflects N episodes, not per-episode bitrate, so it will always win the tie purely by episode count. No test covers a batch-vs-single tie scenario.
confirmed.verdictNote: At HEAD, comparison.go:647-650 and 706-709 tie-break on raw torrent.Size; with neutral BatchPreference a batch passes checkPreference (426-434) with 0 bonus (859-865), and a no-episode season pack is episode-covered (episodeCovered:181-183) with the season demotion gated to expectedSeason>=2 (874-886), so a same-tier batch ties a single and always wins by N-episode total size, contradicting the bitrate rationale. TestAutoSelect_SizeTiebreak (autoselect_test.go:671-684) covers only two singles, and no later commit (38dc857f/e19c8845/66f21c9f/22235bca/9f6e250c all touch other guards) normalizes or gates the tiebreak. Medium severity is correct: degraded ranking, not a broken feature.

confirmed.title: useGetFranchiseRefs' React Query key is (count, min id, max id), not the actual id set — can serve stale/wrong refs for a different list
confirmed.detail: `queryKey: [key, ids.length, ids[0] ?? 0, ids[ids.length - 1] ?? 0]` treats two different id sets that happen to share length + min + max as the same query, so switching between two lists/pages with that coincidence would render one's cached franchise refs (season-grouping badges, TMDB matches) against the other's media ids. The comment calls this 'a cheap stable signature... to avoid churn', but it is not unique to the input.
confirmed.verdictNote: anime_franchise.hooks.ts:45 keys the query as (endpoint, ids.length, min, max) and useServerQuery (requests.ts:275-286) passes queryKey verbatim to useQuery with `data` only in the queryFn closure, so two id sets sharing count+min+max genuinely share one cache entry (staleTime 30min) and even one in-flight fetch. However all consumers in group-seasons.ts resolve refs via Map<mediaId, ref> lookups, so a collision can only leave the differing ids unresolved (entries fail to collapse / no badge, with a title-stem fallback still applying) — refs are never mis-attributed to the wrong media. Real bug but requires a count+min+max coincidence and degrades a presentation-only grouping feature that self-heals after staleTime, so low rather than medium.

confirmed.title: Schedule/missing-episodes/upcoming-episodes caches are gated admin-only, so every non-admin request recomputes from AniList with no caching at all
confirmed.detail: HandleGetAnimeCollectionSchedule, HandleGetMissingEpisodes and HandleGetUpcomingEpisodes only read/write their package-level caches `if sess.IsAdmin`; for any non-admin user the handler always calls GetAnimeAiringSchedule/GetAnimeCollection fresh on every request, since the caches are single global values and per-user keying wasn't added. This avoids cross-user leakage but means non-admin users get zero caching benefit on these AniList-hitting endpoints (repeated remote calls on every page load/poll), unlike the admin path and unlike the (correctly per-user-keyed) tagsCache/mangaTagsCache/statsCache/missedSequelsCache added in the same commit.
confirmed.verdictNote: At HEAD all three handlers read/write their single global caches only `if sess.IsAdmin` (anime_collection.go:242-262, anime_entries.go:483-521/544-567), with no later fork commit adding per-user keying; in-code comments confirm this is the standing tradeoff. Non-admin schedule requests make 1-2 live AniList GraphQL calls each via BuildAnimeAiringSchedule (shared_platform/shared.go:261), and non-admin missing/upcoming requests re-walk every airing media behind a 20/s rateLimiter.Wait() (missing_episodes.go:86/114, upcoming_episodes.go) even on metadata cache hits, adding >1s latency for users with >20 airing entries on a layout-loaded endpoint. One overstatement: GetAnimeCollection(false) is per-user cached (anilist_platform.go:343), so only the schedule endpoint actually re-hits AniList remotely; medium severity stands.

confirmed.title: Repository.GetStreamURL() picks an arbitrary (Go map-iteration-order) user's stream once StreamManagers went per-user
confirmed.detail: 9b969cc3 split the single shared StreamManager into one-per-userID (streamManagers map), but GetStreamURL() (used by nakama host/peer-party relay in handlers/nakama.go:326,372 and the plugin API in plugin/debrid.go:423) still returns 'the first non-empty' stream found by ranging the map — Go map iteration order is randomized, so with two+ users streaming concurrently on the networked deployment, callers that assume a single active stream get a non-deterministic pick. The code comment acknowledges this ('used by single-host features') but the gap is unresolved. Confirmed still present at HEAD (internal/debrid/client/repository.go:490-503), no follow-up commit found via git log -S.
confirmed.evidence: internal/debrid/client/repository.go:490-503 (introduced 9b969cc3, unchanged at HEAD); callers internal/handlers/nakama.go:326,372, internal/plugin/debrid.go:423
confirmed.verdictNote: GetStreamURL (internal/debrid/client/repository.go:493-503) Ranges the per-user result.Map[uint,*StreamManager] and returns the first non-empty URL; reachable at HEAD via the live legacy nakama debrid watch-party chain (watch_party_peer.go:447 → share.go:256 → nakama.go:425 → nakama.go:372) and plugin/debrid.go:423, so with 2+ concurrent user streams a peer can be handed another user's stream. The fork fixed only the in-server room join path with user-scoped GetUserStreamShare (nakama_rooms.go:255); no commit after 9b969cc3 fixes GetStreamURL itself and the legacy routes remain registered (routes.go:618-620).

confirmed.title: watch-room join-stream endpoint has no room-membership or password check (authz bypass)
confirmed.detail: HandleNakamaWatchRoomJoinStream (added in 5e8bc095) only calls guardStreamingUser (requires any logged-in user), unlike HandleNakamaWatchRoomJoin which requires requireRoomIdentity + verifies the room password via WatchRoomHub.JoinRoom. Any authenticated user who knows/discovers a roomId (room IDs + HasPassword/Title/media are broadcast unauthenticated-per-user via HandleNakamaWatchRoomList) can POST /nakama/watch-room/join-stream for a room they never joined and never supplied a password for, and it will fetch and reuse the controller's already-resolved debrid CDN link (GetUserStreamShare) to start a stream. This persists unchanged to HEAD (fd2ffc25) — the current handler still has no participant/password check.
confirmed.evidence: internal/handlers/nakama_rooms.go:211-232 (current), vs internal/nakama/watch_room.go JoinRoom password check at :210-232 (as of c4bef93d); introduced 5e8bc095
confirmed.verdictNote: Confirmed at HEAD: HandleNakamaWatchRoomJoinStream (nakama_rooms.go:211-289) gates only on guardStreamingUser (dataUserID!=0), never checks room.Participants membership or the room password, whereas JoinRoom (watch_room.go:345-390) enforces hashRoomPassword; StreamInfo(roomID) exposes controller stream state with no membership check and roomIds are enumerable via HandleNakamaWatchRoomList. Not critical, though: RoomCard (watch_room.go:183-195) already broadcasts MediaId/EpisodeNumber/Title/HasPassword to every authenticated user, the bypass never adds the caller to Participants (no sync/control/room-integrity compromise), and each peer resolves its own fresh link (no contention on the controller). The only new capability is piggybacking the controller's specific resolved release, content a same-server authenticated user can already stream via normal debrid endpoints, so blast radius is medium, not critical.

confirmed.title: Global MaxConnsPerHost: 8 cap on the shared CDN proxy client may serialize concurrent range requests across users
confirmed.detail: 17f118ea adds MaxConnsPerHost: 8 to the single process-wide videoProxyClient used by every user's directstream range fetches to the debrid CDN host. On the networked multi-user deployment, several concurrent viewers (each opening multiple ranged requests for buffering/seeking) can exceed 8 in-flight connections to the same CDN hostname, causing later requests to queue rather than proceed — the commit's own comment flags this as a guess ('tune if it ever blocks legit reads'). No adaptive/per-user scoping was added alongside it.
confirmed.verdictNote: httpstream.go:54-67 at HEAD still has the shared videoProxyClient with MaxConnsPerHost:8 and ForceAttemptHTTP2:false, used by all users' proxy-mode serves (httpstream.go:518), warms (httpstream_warm.go:203/313) and prewarms (manager.go:346), and proxy serves hold connections for a range's whole live-tee duration — so >8 in-flight to one CDN host does queue cross-user requests behind long-held streams. However the finding's 'no scoping was added' is stale: 70d262c0 (v3.9.1) added cdngate.go, a per-link semaphore (cap 2, env-tunable) at every videoProxyClient call site, and direct-CDN mode (clientStreamUrl) plus DefaultClient-based chunked readers move most traffic off this transport, so hitting the cap now needs 5+ concurrent proxy-mode streams whose distinct links share one CDN hostname. Mechanism confirmed, but at this deployment's scale with the later mitigations the realistic impact is minor, hence severity lowered to low.

confirmed.title: TorBox fileIdCache sync.Map has no eviction/TTL/cap
confirmed.detail: db1a4b59 adds fileIdCache sync.Map on the TorBox client, keyed torrentID|shortName -> numeric file id, written on every GetTorrentDownloadUrl resolve and (per a later commit, 33c0197a) primed on every readiness poll, with no TTL, cap, or delete path anywhere in the file. On a long-running networked server this grows for the life of the process across every distinct torrent+file resolved by any user. The sibling infoCache added in 33c0197a got a hard cap with whole-map-flush eviction, but fileIdCache did not receive the same treatment and is still unbounded at HEAD.
confirmed.evidence: internal/debrid/torbox/torbox.go:39-43 (fileIdCache sync.Map, db1a4b59); :555 and :571 (Load/Store, no eviction); confirmed still present unbounded at current HEAD via grep
confirmed.verdictNote: At HEAD, torbox.go:43 declares fileIdCache sync.Map with Store at :489 (readiness poll primes every file of a ready torrent) and :571, Load at :555, and no Delete/TTL/cap anywhere in the package, while sibling infoCache has TTL=1h + cap=4096 + flush (:133-144); the provider is a process-lifetime singleton (client/repository.go:202), so the map is genuinely unbounded. However each entry is only a short "torrentID|shortName" -> numeric-id string (~hundreds of bytes), growth is bounded by distinct torrents resolved × files, and keys never go stale-wrong (TorBox IDs unique, file lists immutable) — realistic accumulation is single-digit MB/year on an 8GB Pi, so severity is low (minor leak), not medium.

confirmed.title: "Merge debrid pill into loading screen" edits an unmounted component / reads a shadow atom that is never written
confirmed.detail: a8d1104d's web half wires `VideoCoreLoadingScreen` to `__debridstream_stateAtom` imported from `debrid-stream-overlay.tsx`, but `DebridStreamOverlay` (the only writer of that atom) is never rendered anywhere in the app (repo-wide grep at HEAD finds only its own definition). The actually-mounted pill (`PlaybackPlayPill` in `playback-play-pill.tsx`, rendered unconditionally by `main-layout.tsx`) uses a *different* atom of the exact same exported name `__debridstream_stateAtom` defined in its own file, fed by `handle-debrid-stream.ts`. Consequently the loading screen's `debridState` is always null (detailed message/torrent-name never shows, falls back to the coarse `loadingState`), and `PlaybackPlayPill` never consults `vc_loadingScreenVisibleAtom`, so the floating pill still stacks over the new loading screen during the debrid "downloading" phase — the exact behavior the commit message says it fixed.
confirmed.evidence: seanime-web/src/app/(main)/_features/video-core/video-core-loading-screen.tsx:3,56 (imports __debridstream_stateAtom from debrid-stream-overlay.tsx); seanime-web/src/app/(main)/entry/_containers/debrid-stream/debrid-stream-overlay.tsx:24 (separate atom instance, component never referenced elsewhere per grep); seanime-web/src/app/(main)/entry/_containers/torrent-stream/playback-play-pill.tsx:35,52,353 (the real, mounted pill's atom + isDebridLoading gating, no loadingScreenVisible check); commit a8d1104d
confirmed.verdictNote: At HEAD, video-core-loading-screen.tsx:3,56 reads the __debridstream_stateAtom defined in debrid-stream-overlay.tsx:24, whose only writer is the DEBRID_STREAM_STATE listener inside DebridStreamOverlay — a component repo-wide grep finds referenced nowhere but its own definition (already true at a8d1104d), so the loading screen's debridState is permanently null and its message/torrentName branches are dead. The actually-mounted PlaybackPlayPill (main-layout.tsx:61) uses its own same-named atom (playback-play-pill.tsx:35, written by its listener at line 270), never reads vc_loadingScreenVisibleAtom (only reader is the unmounted overlay at line 36/125), and its shouldHideFloating (lines 363-367) is false while isPlayerLoading, so the z-[1000] pill stacks over the mounted loading screen during debrid downloading — exactly what the commit claimed to fix; later commits a5ddaf67/ce4181ac did not change this wiring. Downgraded to medium: no functionality lost (detail still visible via the pill, playback unaffected) — purely presentational regression of a cosmetic merge feature.

confirmed.title: Per-session MpvCore/Coordinator goroutines are never stopped when a session is evicted
confirmed.detail: 57ad877d gives every `UserSession` its own `mpvcore.MpvCore` and `mediacore.Coordinator` (in addition to the pre-existing `videoCore`), each spawning goroutines via `mpvcore.New`/`Start()` (`go mc.listenToClientEvents()` ranges over a subscriber channel that is only closed by re-subscribing under the same key, never explicitly). `a.sessions.Delete(userID)` (called on relink/logout in `LoginUserToAnilist`/`logoutUserFromAnilist`) just drops the map entry — no `Stop()`/`Shutdown()` is called on the evicted session's videoCore/mpvCore/mediaCoord/directStream, so their listener goroutines block forever on channels that will never receive or close again. This pre-existed for videoCore alone; this commit doubles the leaked-goroutine surface per relink/logout cycle on a long-running networked server.
confirmed.evidence: internal/core/session.go:150-181 (mpvCore/mediaCoord construction in ensureModules), internal/core/session.go:523,546 (a.sessions.Delete with no per-session Stop call), internal/mpvcore/mpvcore.go:424-425 (`for raw := range mc.clientPlayerEventSubscriber.Channel` — no stopCh select), internal/events/websocket.go:454-459 (SubscribeToClientMpvCoreEvents just `.Set()`s a fresh subscriber, never closes/removes the old one)
confirmed.verdictNote: At HEAD, session.go:150-180 builds per-session MpvCore/Coordinator whose mpvcore.New→Start() spawns listenToClientEvents (mpvcore.go:425, bare `range` over the subscriber channel, no stopCh) plus a dispatcher goroutine, and sessions.Delete (session.go:523,546) performs no teardown — grep confirms session.go never calls the existing MpvCore.Shutdown() or any Unsubscribe. The subscriber is registered under key `mpvcore:u%d` via SubscribeToClientMpvCoreEvents (websocket.go:454-460) whose resultmap Set is a plain sync.Map Store that never closes the displaced channel, and UnsubscribeFromClientEvents is only ever called with plugin ids (plugin/ui/ui.go:110), so evicted goroutines park forever. Eviction is also automatic: logoutUserFromAnilist is the AnilistPlatform invalid-token callback (session.go:478), so a client polling with a revoked token leaks one full module graph per rebuild cycle.

confirmed.title: New isDownloaded install-prompt effect is suppressed exactly when disableUpdateCheck is set — one of the two scenarios the commit says it fixes
confirmed.detail: a66bc395's fix is meant to surface the install modal when electron-updater finishes downloading despite the server-side check reporting nothing, explicitly citing 'external-server mode, disableUpdateCheck' as repro conditions. But the new effect returns early `if (serverStatus?.settings?.library?.disableUpdateCheck) return` before checking `isDownloaded`, so for users with that setting on, the modal still never opens even after a real download completes. electron-updater's own polling (`autoUpdater.checkForUpdatesAndNotify()` in main.js) runs unconditionally in the Electron main process, independent of this renderer-side setting, so the silent-download bug the commit targets is left unfixed for that exact case.
confirmed.verdictNote: At HEAD, electron-update-modal.tsx:152 returns early on disableUpdateCheck before the isDownloaded check at :153, and the render guard at :258 also hides the sidebar entry, so no install UI ever appears for that setting; no commit after a66bc395 touched the file. Meanwhile main.js:1789 calls autoUpdater.checkForUpdatesAndNotify() unconditionally with autoDownload=true (:1634) and zero disableUpdateCheck references, so update-downloaded IPC (:856-864) still fires and the download completes silently — exactly the commit-message scenario left unfixed. Impact is softened by autoInstallOnAppQuit=true (:1635, update applies at next quit) plus a toast, so medium is correctly calibrated.

confirmed.title: Durable last-watched store doubles filecache disk I/O on the existing 10s progress-save hot path
confirmed.detail: setLastWatched() (called from UpdateWatchHistoryItem/UpdateExternalPlayerEpisodeWatchHistoryItem/RecordLastWatchedOpen) calls fileCacher.Set() then trimLastWatchedItems(), which calls filecache.GetAll() — every filecache Set/GetAll unconditionally calls CacheStore.saveToFile() (os.Create + full JSON re-encode of the whole bucket, up to 1000 items). This runs on every 10-second periodic progress save and on every episode open for every actively-playing session, in addition to the pre-existing resume-store save that already did this — effectively doubling the full-bucket disk-write pattern on the hottest path in the playback lifecycle.
confirmed.verdictNote: At HEAD, setLastWatched (history.go:461-470) runs on every UpdateWatchHistoryItem, which mediacore fires per StatusEvent with no throttle (mediacore.go:485-490) — and both web video-core and MpvCore clients emit status every 1000ms (video-core-events.ts:424-433, mpv-core-player-inner.tsx:1204), so the cadence is 1s, not 10s. Each call adds THREE full-bucket saveToFile rewrites of the up-to-1000-item _lw bucket (Set at filecache.go:126, plus GetAll saving twice: Range filecache.go:156 and GetAll filecache.go:201) on top of the pre-existing resume-store write — a 4x full-file-rewrite pattern per tick, per active session (per-user buckets, manager.go:71). No later fork commit debounces or fixes it (a75a7e30 introduced it; nothing after touches lastWatched/filecache).

confirmed.title: Upstream StreamAutoSelectStatus (play-pill candidate feed) wired to admin-scoped event plane, dead for non-admin users
confirmed.detail: The resolution kept upstream's new OnStatus wiring verbatim — `opts.WSEventManager.SendEvent(events.StreamAutoSelectStatus, ...)` — but in the fork that manager is a.adminEvents (scoped to the admin user + unidentified clients), and the resolution did not route it through the fork's per-user SessionEventsFunc pattern used for all other stream events (`s.ev(opts)`). On the networked multi-user Pi, non-admin users never receive the auto-select searching/ranking/analyzing steps or candidate list (playback-play-pill.tsx:312 listener stays silent), while the admin's client receives every other user's auto-select status unfiltered (minor cross-user leak of what others are starting, and possible pill-state cross-talk). Core stream status still flows per-user via DebridStreamState, so this degrades rather than breaks the loading UI. Same wiring exists in torrentstream (admin-owned infra, arguably by fork policy) — the debrid one is the user-facing gap.
confirmed.evidence: 20596e8b resolution hunk; live at HEAD internal/debrid/client/repository.go:140-142 (WSEventManager=adminEvents at internal/core/modules.go:346), internal/torrentstream/repository.go:131-133, consumer seanime-web/src/app/(main)/entry/_containers/torrent-stream/playback-play-pill.tsx:312
confirmed.verdictNote: At HEAD, repository.go:140-142 sends StreamAutoSelectStatus via opts.WSEventManager = a.adminEvents (modules.go:346), whose SendEvent → SendEventToUserOrUnscoped(adminID) (websocket.go:236-250) reaches only the admin's connections on the networked server, while the same constructor's SessionEventsFunc is used for 20+ per-user DebridStreamState sends (stream.go:323 ev()) but not for OnStatus. FindBestTorrent (autoselect.go:199, called from finder.go:116 in the per-user stream-start path) emits searching/ranking/analyzing + candidates for every user's auto-select, so non-admin pills (playback-play-pill.tsx:312, no payload filtering) never receive it and the admin's pill receives all users' statuses including candidate torrent names. Wiring is upstream b09257d9 merged in 20596e8b; no fork commit after 2026-07-04 fixes the routing.

confirmed.title: Nil playbackCtx race can panic the server via context.WithCancel(nil) in StartSubtitleStreamP
confirmed.detail: releaseCurrentStreamLocked sets m.playbackCtx = nil (stream.go:310, 380) while in-flight range handlers and player-event handlers read it WITHOUT playbackMu and pass it to StartSubtitleStreamP, which calls context.WithCancel(playbackCtx) with no nil guard (subtitles.go:377). httpstream.go:460 spawns the subtitle kick in a bare goroutine with no recover, so hitting the window during an episode switch concurrent with a seek storm panics the whole process; the SeekedEvent path (go m.startSubtitleStreamForTime, stream.go:578) has the same unsynchronized read. Window is narrow (request must pass the manager route before the release) but episode switches while the old player is still issuing ranges are routine in this deployment. Upstream code from 8b5c6bba, present since the v3.9.0 merge; not in fable-audit.md.
confirmed.verdictNote: At HEAD, releaseCurrentStreamLocked/discardCurrentStreamLocked nil m.playbackCtx (stream.go:310,380) while httpstream.go:460 reads it without playbackMu and passes it via a bare unrecovered goroutine into StartSubtitleStreamP, whose context.WithCancel(playbackCtx) at subtitles.go:377 has no nil guard and panics on nil parent; the event-loop paths (stream.go:525 unlocks before the switch; :546-570, :577 -> subtitles.go:227-253) read it unsynchronized too. The codebase nil-guards this same field at torrentstream.go:234-236, httpstream.go:357-360, httpstream_warm.go:119, and subtitles.go:289, proving nil is a known reachable state at unsynchronized read points, yet the subtitle-kick paths are unguarded and no fork commit fixed them. An unrecovered goroutine panic crashes the whole multi-user server process, which is 'critical' per the rubric (crash), hence the severity correction.

confirmed.title: serveHandoff dead-warm fallback has a hole: partial cached span < 1MiB chunk yields an empty-206 reconnect loop instead of CDN redirect
confirmed.detail: The 'warm dead' 302-to-CDN fallback only fires when CachedSpanFrom(ra.Start) == 0 (httpstream_warm.go:77). If warmRange failed after a partial fill, a request whose offset has 0 < cached span < the 1MiB serve chunk enters the serve loop, IsRangeAvailable fails, warmFailed.Load() returns immediately — a 206 advertising the full range with zero payload bytes — and the player's reconnect at the same offset repeats identically forever (the exact loop the code comment says the guard exists to prevent). Affects MpvCore direct-handoff mode only and needs a mid-window warm failure (CDN error after retries exhausted); a user retry creates a fresh stream/warm and usually recovers.
confirmed.evidence: internal/directstream/httpstream_warm.go:77 (guard), 115-127 (wait loop returns 0 bytes); fork feature commits 53473a90/0e2a0da4
confirmed.verdictNote: At HEAD, httpstream_warm.go:77 only redirects to CDN when warmFailed AND CachedSpanFrom(ra.Start)==0; a partial fill (warmRange writes 256KiB chunks via WriteCacheAt before markWarmFailed on a mid-body error, lines 231-246) leaves 0<span<1MiB, so the serve loop's IsRangeAvailable (requires one piece covering the full chunk, filestream.go:203-209) fails and the warmFailed check at line 118 returns after the 206 headers (line 93) with zero payload — the player reconnects at the same offset into identical state, since warmOnce never retries and no other path fills the cache in handoff mode (serveHandoff returns at httpstream.go:473; readahead is opt-in/off). No fork commit after 53473a90 touches this logic; medium severity is correct (MpvCore direct-handoff only, needs a retries-exhausted mid-window CDN failure, user retry creates a fresh stream and recovers).

confirmed.title: capturePrewarmWindows/fetchRangeBytes has no timeout while holding a per-token CDN gate slot — a stalled CDN read leaks the slot indefinitely
confirmed.detail: fetchRangeBytes (manager.go:340) issues an up-to-48MiB GET with no request context and videoProxyClient has no Client.Timeout, so a CDN that stalls mid-body (without erroring) hangs io.ReadAll forever. The caller capturePrewarmWindows holds a tryAcquire'd per-token gate slot for the whole capture (defer release), so one hang permanently consumes 1 of the link's 2 slots; after the 1h/2h cache TTLs expire a re-prewarm can hang the second slot, at which point that link's serve path blocks at cdnTokenGateInst.acquire and the episode won't start. Contrast: warmRange is bounded by playbackCtx and the metadata parse by metadataParseTimeout — this is the one unbounded CDN read on the prewarm path.
confirmed.evidence: internal/directstream/manager.go:340 (fetchRangeBytes), 315-338 (capturePrewarmWindows tryAcquire/defer release); internal/directstream/httpstream.go:54-67 (videoProxyClient has no timeout)
confirmed.verdictNote: fetchRangeBytes (manager.go:341-354) builds a context-less request and io.ReadAll's the body via videoProxyClient, which has no Client.Timeout/ResponseHeaderTimeout (httpstream.go:54-67), so a stalled-but-alive CDN body blocks forever while capturePrewarmWindows holds a tryAcquire'd slot of the 2-cap per-token gate via defer (manager.go:315-320, cdngate.go:27) in a detached goroutine (manager.go:199) with no watchdog. The serve path blocks at acquire(r.Context()) on the same key (httpstream.go:484), and cdnTokenKey strips the auth query (cdngate.go:39-44) so a re-resolved token maps to the same leaked semaphore. The hazard was explicitly recognized and bounded for the metadata parse on this same prewarm path (metadataParseTimeout, manager.go:171-175) but not applied to fetchRangeBytes.

confirmed.title: Extension install/uninstall/edit reachable by any non-admin (and anon-with-password), not admin-gated
confirmed.detail: The /api/v1/extensions/external/* routes (install, install-repository, uninstall, edit-payload, reload, disabled) carry no AdminOnly/UserOnly middleware in routes.go and rely only on the inline guardPrivilegedExtensionManagement. That guard resolves to canUsePrivilegedExtensionManagement -> isTrustedRequest(req, serverPassword), which returns true whenever a server password is set (local_security.go:278-287). So on the networked multi-user server every logged-in regular user, and even a browse-only anon that merely knows the shared server password, can install an extension from an arbitrary manifest URL into the shared repo (remote code into the plugin sandbox affecting all users) or uninstall/disable others' extensions. This contradicts the fork's admin-managed-extensions design and the anon-browse-only guarantee (f018cad4).
confirmed.verdictNote: Traced at HEAD: routes.go:514-539 attaches no AdminOnly/UserOnly to the /extensions/external/* routes; each handler (extensions.go:61,89,116,144,162,185,209) guards only via guardPrivilegedExtensionManagement, which in the deployment's default (non-strict) security mode returns canUsePrivilegedExtensionManagement -> isTrustedRequest, and isTrustedRequest (local_security.go:278-281) returns true unconditionally whenever a server password is set. The outer gate (server_auth_middleware.go:37) only requires the shared X-Seanime-Token, so every non-admin logged-in user and any anon who merely knows the shared password can install remote extension code into the shared repo or uninstall/disable others' extensions — no admin identity is ever checked on this path. This is a missing authorization check enabling non-admin remote-code install into a shared server-side plugin sandbox, which the rubric classes as a critical security hole rather than high.

confirmed.title: /status leaks torrent-client passwords and infra paths to non-admin users
confirmed.detail: NewStatus redacts only DebridSettings.ApiKey for non-admins (status.go:175-179), but the same response still returns status.Settings.Torrent with plaintext QBittorrentPassword/TransmissionPassword, plus status.TorrentstreamSettings (download dir, hosts) and status.MediastreamSettings (ffmpeg/ffprobe paths, pre-transcode dir). Every non-admin user, and any anon that knows the shared server password (the full-status path runs once past isStrictModeSensitive), receives these admin infra secrets. The fork wanted server/infra settings hidden from regular users; the redaction is incomplete.
confirmed.verdictNote: At HEAD, NewStatus (internal/handlers/status.go:144-179) redacts only DebridSettings.ApiKey for non-admins while still returning Settings.Torrent with QBittorrentPassword/TransmissionPassword (plain json tags, models.go:254-278), MediastreamSettings (FfmpegPath/FfprobePath/PreTranscodeLibraryDir, models.go:524-541), and TorrentstreamSettings (DownloadDir/TorrentClientHost, models.go:547-568) as raw structs. No custom MarshalJSON exists in internal/database/models, UserOverrides.ApplyTo never strips the Torrent block, and the code's own comment admits other secrets are only "gated behind hidden tabs on the client" — client-side hiding, not a wire-level boundary. Medium is correctly calibrated: exposure is limited to password-authenticated non-admin users and the leaked credentials are secondary infra (torrent client, likely localhost-bound), not the redacted debrid key.

confirmed.title: upstream #866: Browser direct play only works for H.264+AAC files (malformed mimeCodec strings)
confirmed.detail: Confirmed present at internal/mediastream/videofile/info.go:398 - `ret += fmt.Sprintf(".L%02X.BO", stream.Level)` for HEVC uses hex level + literal letter O instead of digit 0 (should be decimal + `.B0`), so HEVC never satisfies the browser's canPlayType() check and always falls back to transcoding; AV1 level and Opus/VP9 mappings have the same class of bug per the report. Directly affects local-library browser playback (part of our deployment profile) on any non-H.264 file.
confirmed.verdictNote: info.go:398 at HEAD still emits `.L%02X.BO` for HEVC (hex level + letter O instead of hex constraint byte B0), producing e.g. `hvc1.2.4.L78.BO` which fails RFC 6381 parsing, and AV1 at line 424 uses hex where av01 requires decimal level. The client only direct-plays when `video.canPlayType(mimeCodec) === "probably"` (mediastream/page.tsx:162 + video-core/_lib/hooks.ts:10), so malformed strings force transcode for every non-H.264 local file. No fork commit touched info.go (last change is upstream af208f47), so the upstream bug is fully present; high severity is apt given forced software transcode on the Pi 5.

plausible.title: Server self-update / release download reachable by any non-admin (and anon-with-password)
plausible.detail: /api/v1/install-update and /api/v1/download-release are gated only by guardPrivilegedLocalExecution, which is a no-op once a server password is configured (isTrustedRequest returns true, local_security.go:435-449 + :278-287) and no AdminOnly middleware is applied in routes.go. On the networked server, any password-holder (regular user or browse-only anon) can trigger a binary self-update + restart or force the host to download release files, an availability/integrity vector that should require the admin role.

plausible.title: Watch-room player sync is dead when Denshi plays via MpvCore (the current default)
plausible.detail: useWatchRoomPlayerSync bridges the room relay exclusively through vc_globalVideoElement (written only by VideoCoreGlobalBridge in video-core.tsx) and nativePlayer_stateAtom (written only by native-player.tsx, which renders VideoCore only); MpvCore playback flows over WSEvents.MPVCORE into separate mc_* atoms and never touches either. A Denshi-on-MpvCore room member therefore emits nothing as driver (no heartbeat/play/pause/seek/stop, so followers never auto-start) and applies nothing as follower (videoElement null gate at nakama-room-sync.ts:385; the p.stopped/room-closed teardown via nativePlayer_terminateRequestedAtom is consumed only by native-player.tsx:254 so MpvCore also ignores stops). This worked pre-merge when Denshi used the videocore native player (live-tested June); the v3.9.0 merge (20596e8b) introduced MpvCore and v3.9.4 (fd2ffc25) made it the Denshi default, silently killing room sync for the fork's primary desktop client. Browser/VideoCore and Tenji participants are unaffected.
plausible.evidence: seanime-web/src/app/(main)/_features/nakama/nakama-room-sync.ts:79-87,262,385; seanime-web/src/app/(main)/_features/video-core/video-core.tsx:208-223 (sole bridge writer); seanime-web/src/app/(main)/_features/mpv-core/mpv-core.tsx:555-556 (WSEvents.MPVCORE, separate mc_* atoms); seanime-web/src/app/(main)/_features/native-player/native-player.tsx:254,284; commits 20596e8b, fd2ffc25

plausible.title: Single global nakama Manager binds the admin's MediacoreCoordinator on the multi-user fork
plausible.detail: nakama.NewManager is constructed once (modules.go:418-430) with the app-level a.MediacoreCoordinator, but the fork's per-user sessions each have their own Coordinator (57ad877d). All upstream watch-party player control paths (listenToPlaybackAsHost subscription, WatchPartyGenericPlayer pause/resume/seek/terminate, GetActivePlaybackState) therefore see and drive only the admin session's player. A non-admin user's Denshi/videocore playback is invisible to watch-party hosting, and worse, watch-party commands issued while a party is live would target the admin's player, not the acting user's. Fork watch rooms are unaffected (client-relay only, no coordinator use), so impact is limited to the upstream federation watch party on this multi-user deployment.

plausible.title: Loading screen's debrid-status absorption is dead: it reads an atom nothing writes since the v3.9.0 merge
plausible.detail: There are two distinct atoms both named __debridstream_stateAtom: the fork-era one in debrid-stream-overlay.tsx and upstream's in playback-play-pill.tsx. Upstream's v3.9.0 pill redesign (b09257d9, merged via 20596e8b) unmounted DebridStreamOverlay (zero importers at HEAD), so its atom — the one video-core-loading-screen.tsx reads — is never written; handle-debrid-stream.ts writes the pill's atom instead. Result: the a8d1104d 'merge debrid pill into loading screen' feature is fully broken — detailed progress ('Downloading torrent: 45%') and torrentName never appear on the Stremio loading screen (a5ddaf67's recency logic operates on a permanently-null channel), and the pill-hiding half is dead too since vc_loadingScreenVisibleAtom's only reader is the unmounted overlay, so the upstream floating pill stacks on top of the loading screen during starts. Fix is one import swap (loading screen → pill's atom) plus a vc_loadingScreenVisibleAtom check in PlaybackPlayPill's showFloatingPill.
plausible.evidence: seanime-web/src/app/(main)/_features/video-core/video-core-loading-screen.tsx:3,56; seanime-web/src/app/(main)/entry/_containers/debrid-stream/debrid-stream-overlay.tsx:24 (no importers at HEAD); seanime-web/src/app/(main)/entry/_containers/torrent-stream/playback-play-pill.tsx:35,270; seanime-web/src/app/(main)/entry/_containers/debrid-stream/_lib/handle-debrid-stream.ts:14; commits 20596e8b, a8d1104d, a5ddaf67

plausible.title: Debrid auto-resume after server restart never arms for MpvCore playback (Denshi's primary player)
plausible.detail: useDebridReconnectResume (cbeb92c6) watches only nativePlayer_stateAtom and is mounted only in native-player.tsx; with MpvPrism enabled post-v3.9, Denshi playback runs through mpvCore_stateAtom, so streamActive is always false and droppedWhileActiveRef never arms. A mid-play server deploy/crash therefore kills MpvCore playback with no re-issue of the last DebridStartStream, though lastDebridStreamStartAtom is still populated by handle-debrid-stream for that path. Browser/VideoCore playback still resumes correctly. Fix: also read mpvCore_stateAtom (active + playbackInfo) or mount an equivalent hook in the MpvCore player.
plausible.evidence: seanime-web/src/app/(main)/entry/_containers/debrid-stream/_lib/handle-debrid-reconnect.ts:24,32-53; seanime-web/src/app/(main)/_features/native-player/native-player.tsx:31; no useDebridReconnectResume/mpvCore_stateAtom coverage under _features/mpv-core; commits cbeb92c6 (feature), 20596e8b (MpvCore arrival)

plausible.title: Durable _lw store rewrites its whole bucket file 3x and scans all items on every 1 Hz progress tick
plausible.detail: setLastWatched calls trimLastWatchedItems unconditionally on every upsert, and trim does filecache.GetAll over the entire _lw bucket; GetAll itself rewrites the bucket file twice (Range saves, then GetAll saves again) on top of the Set's own full-file rewrite. Since UpdateWatchHistoryItem mirrors into _lw and the browser/Denshi player sends video-status every 1000 ms (video-core-events.ts setInterval 1000 -> mediacore StatusEvent -> UpdateWatchHistoryItem), each active stream causes 3 full rewrites of watch_history_uN_lw.cache plus a marshal+unmarshal of every stored item (cap 1000) every second, sustained for hours on the Pi's SD card. The upstream resume-store pattern the fork mirrored only trims when a new item was inserted (history.go:214 'if added'); the fork's trim runs on every tick even when the store is nowhere near MaxLastWatchedItems. Fix shape: gate trimLastWatchedItems on insertion of a new key (or a cheap in-memory count), mirroring the resume-store gate.
plausible.evidence: internal/continuity/history.go:461-469 (unconditional trim), :473-495 (GetAll per call), :199/:409 (mirror on every update); internal/util/filecache/filecache.go:129-157,183-202 (Range+GetAll each saveToFile); internal/mediacore/mediacore.go:485-490,594; seanime-web/src/app/(main)/_features/video-core/video-core-events.ts:424-433; introduced a75a7e30

plausible.title: upstream #868: Subtitle tracks fail with "no file has been loaded" when reopening an episode in the browser player
plausible.detail: Confirmed `useRequestMediastreamMediaContainer` in seanime-web/src/api/hooks/mediastream.hooks.ts:36-41 is a tanstack query wrapping `RequestMediastreamMediaContainer` (a POST with a server-side side effect registering the active file) with no gcTime override, matching the reported race where a cached response lets the player mount and fetch subs before the file is re-registered. Reproduces on reopening a recently-watched local episode or after a server restart with an already-open SPA tab.

plausible.title: upstream #738: Use Nakama Host's anime library for the Schedule Tab when connected as a peer
plausible.detail: Grep for "Schedule" in internal/nakama turns up nothing, consistent with the report that the Schedule tab always reads the peer's own local library rather than the host's shared library when connected via Nakama, which is a core usage mode in our deployment profile (nakama watch rooms).

plausible.title: upstream #679: Auto Downloader should make separate RSS request per anime (Nyaa 75-item feed cap causes missed episodes on non-uptime PCs)
plausible.detail: This is the built-in Go autodownloader (internal/library/autodownloader), a separate code path from the external Nyaa/AIOStreams addon our multi-season-pack fix touched (per memory multiseason-pack-and-nyaa-addon-deploy.md); the described single-feed-with-75-item-cap design is a plausible real limitation for any Denshi/desktop user who doesn't keep the app running 24/7.

plausible.title: upstream #227: feature request: add Usenet support for TorBox Debrid
plausible.detail: We use TorBox as primary debrid provider (internal/debrid/torbox/torbox.go); grep confirms no usenet handling exists anywhere in internal/, so the gap applies equally to our fork. Purely additive feature, not a regression.

plausible.title: upstream #378: feature request: Support for .strm files
plausible.detail: Request explicitly cites TorBox debrid workflows (storageless library via remote-URL text files), overlapping our debrid-streaming + magnet-to-library feature area; grep found no .strm handling in internal/library, so the gap exists in our fork too. Different mechanism than our directstream architecture, so not a duplicate of existing work.

plausible.area: videocore / browser player (also relevant to Denshi MpvCore)
plausible.title: upstream #490: feature request: Add volume boost in the new built in media player
plausible.detail: Tagged 'videocore' upstream, which we ship and use as our primary browser-side player (Denshi/MpvCore also plays low-volume BDRip audio per our own memory notes on audio tracks); grep for gain-node/volume-boost in seanime-web found nothing, so cap is still 100% in our fork.

refuted.title: cancelStream's 'clear the share triple together' fix still has an early-return gap on GetProvider() error
refuted.detail: b1885ef3 added `s.setCurrentTorrentItemId(""); s.setCurrentFileId("")` at the very end of cancelStream to close the exact torn-selection footgun flagged in fable-audit.md §9.6, but `s.setCurrentStreamUrl("")` still runs early (line 1022) while the two id-clears sit after the `opts.RemoveTorrent` block, which contains a `return` on `s.repository.GetProvider()` error (stream.go:1027-1031). If GetProvider fails while a torrent removal was requested, the function returns with streamUrl already empty but torrentItemId/fileId still stale -- reproducing the same torn-selection state the fix's comment claims to prevent.
refuted.verdictNote: The early-return gap exists syntactically (stream.go:1030 skips the id-clears at 1041-1042), but the resulting state (streamUrl="" + stale ids) is unobservable: the only readers of the ids are shareSnapshot's two consumers, GetUserStreamShare (repository.go:526) and RefreshStreamUrl (repository.go:557), and both return "no active stream" when streamUrl is empty. Stale ids can never pair with a live URL either — every start path unconditionally overwrites torrentItemId before setting the URL (startStream stream.go:618, including "" for direct-StreamUrl; preload path stream.go:1499). Reachability is also near-nil: GetProvider only fails with ErrProviderNotSet (repository.go:256-259), i.e., debrid unconfigured mid-stream, and the sole residual effect is a benign DeleteTorrent retry on a later cancel.

lowUnverified.title: filter()/sort() legacy shims never received the CleanReleaseName lowerName fix that buildCandidates() got
lowUnverified.detail: 4fd80519 fixed buildCandidates() to build candidate.lowerName from util.CleanReleaseName(t.Name) instead of the raw name (the GB-size-as-English bug), but the parallel `filter` and `sort` functions (explicitly commented 'shim for testing or legacy usage') still build lowerName from strings.ToLower(t.Name) with size tokens intact. They are only referenced from autoselect_test.go, not production code, so the bug doesn't reach users, but the fix is inconsistently applied across the file's three candidate-building code paths.
lowUnverified.evidence: internal/torrents/autoselect/comparison.go:166 (buildCandidates, fixed) vs lines 288 and 306 (filter/sort shims, still raw name); only callers are internal/torrents/autoselect/autoselect_test.go:156,242,261,294,321

lowUnverified.title: An extra reachable from multiple main-line members via different AniList relation types gets a nondeterministic tag/IsExtra classification
lowUnverified.detail: ec4ade79's extras loop does `tree.Range(func(_ int, v *CompleteAnime) bool { for edge := range v.GetRelations()... addMember(node, rel) } })`; `addMember` is first-add-wins, but `tree` is backed by `result.Map` (sync.Map-like), whose Range order is not guaranteed by Go. If two members link the same node via different relation types (e.g. one as SIDE_STORY, another as PARENT), which relation/tag wins depends on map iteration order and can vary between the 24h-TTL cache misses.
lowUnverified.evidence: internal/handlers/anime_franchise.go:96-112 (addMember 'first add wins' + tree.Range loop); internal/api/anilist/media_tree.go:14-16 (CompleteAnimeRelationTree wraps result.Map)

lowUnverified.title: Global mergedSeason atom is cleared in a post-render effect, so a franchise-to-franchise navigation can render MergedSeasonSection with a new rootId but stale season/tmdb for one cycle
lowUnverified.detail: `__entry_mergedSeasonAtom` is a module-level jotai atom holding `{season, tmdb}` set by season-switcher.tsx; anime-entry-page.tsx resets it via `useEffect(() => setMergedSeason(null), [mediaId])`, which runs after the render that already mounted `<MergedSeasonSection rootId={Number(mediaId)} seasonNumber={mergedSeason.season} tmdb={mergedSeason.tmdb}/>` with the previous entry's season/tmdb. Only navigation paths that go through season-switcher's own `go()`/`selectSeason()` clear the atom synchronously before navigating; any other route to a new entry (search, library, back button) leaves the stale value for one render, firing a mismatched useGetMergedSeason(rootId, staleSeason, staleTmdb) request that is immediately superseded.

lowUnverified.title: Next-episode preload trigger moved from 80% playback progress to ~3 seconds into playback
lowUnverified.detail: video-core.tsx's preload-next-episode effect changed from firing at `percent >= 0.8` to `v.currentTime >= 3`, explicitly so 'uncached torrents get the whole episode to download in the background' during the current episode. This means a torrent add + stream resolution (and, per PrewarmMetadata=true above, a metadata/font prewarm) now starts for every episode almost immediately after playback begins, rather than near its end, increasing debrid/bandwidth usage for users who frequently stop watching partway through an episode.

lowUnverified.title: Friendly duplicate-username check is a non-atomic check-then-insert (TOCTOU)
lowUnverified.detail: CreateUser looks up the username, and if not found proceeds to Create — two concurrent admin add-user requests for the same username can both pass the check and race on the underlying UNIQUE constraint, defeating the friendly-error purpose (one request would then surface the raw DB error again). Low impact since only reachable via the admin-only /user/register endpoint and the DB constraint still prevents a duplicate row.

lowUnverified.title: LibrarySettings still carries GroupSeasons/HideFranchiseSpinoffs/HideFranchiseRecaps fields that are no longer read by the frontend
lowUnverified.detail: 2d7c7f63 moved season-grouping to the per-user Theme model and switched every frontend read site (group-seasons.ts, season-switcher.tsx) to serverStatus.themeSettings.*, and removed the fields from UserOverrides/ApplyTo/ExtractUserOverrides, but the original LibrarySettings.GroupSeasons/HideFranchiseSpinoffs/HideFranchiseRecaps columns in models.go were left in place — dead/unused data that could confuse future readers of the settings API payload.

lowUnverified.title: Package-level `accountCache` var gains an additional writer (UpsertAccountForUser) with no mutex
lowUnverified.detail: accountCache is a plain package-level *models.Account (pre-existing, not introduced here) read/written without any lock; 526843bb adds a new write path (UpsertAccountForUser sets accountCache when the linked user is the admin) alongside the existing GetAccount()/UpsertAccount() writers, broadening concurrent-write surface on a server that now expects multiple simultaneous per-user requests (login/logout races could interleave with a concurrent admin GetAccount read).

lowUnverified.title: f018cad4's anon-cannot-stream guard covers /mediastream/request but not /mediastream/preload
lowUnverified.detail: f018cad4 adds guardStreamingUser to HandleRequestMediastreamMediaContainer (the container 'start') but HandlePreloadMediastreamMediaContainer, which triggers the same preload/transcode work, keeps only guardMediaConsumption (the shared-password check). An anonymous client that knows the server password but has no user session can therefore still trigger mediastream preload/transcode work, inconsistent with the commit's stated intent ('anonymous users may browse but not stream'). Still present at HEAD.
lowUnverified.evidence: internal/handlers/mediastream.go:75-78 (guardStreamingUser added) vs. internal/handlers/mediastream.go:133-137 (HandlePreloadMediastreamMediaContainer, no guardStreamingUser) — verified unchanged at HEAD

lowUnverified.title: ResolveDirectStreamManager/ResolveDirectStreamManagerWithAttachment read UserSession.directStream outside the sync.Once that guards its construction
lowUnverified.detail: dd6e63a0 introduces App.ResolveDirectStreamManager, which iterates a.sessions.Range and reads s.directStream directly. That field is only lazily built (and safely published) via s.modulesOnce.Do inside ensureModules(), reached through the DirectStream()/Playback()/etc. accessors — never via a call to Do() from ResolveDirectStreamManager itself. A concurrent goroutine resolving a serve request this way has no happens-before guarantee (per the Go memory model) with the goroutine that built the session's DirectStream manager, i.e. a data race on a pointer field, even though in practice it is unlikely to surface as visible corruption on typical hardware. Same pattern in the later-added ResolveDirectStreamManagerWithAttachment.
lowUnverified.evidence: internal/core/session.go:339-359 (ResolveDirectStreamManager, a.sessions.Range reading s.directStream raw); internal/core/session.go:113-216 (s.directStream only written inside s.modulesOnce.Do)

lowUnverified.title: requireRoomIdentity() is applied to Create/Join but not to Leave/SetControl/ForceTracks/AutoSkip
lowUnverified.detail: internal/handlers/nakama_rooms.go calls h.requireRoomIdentity(c) in HandleNakamaWatchRoomCreate and HandleNakamaWatchRoomJoin, but HandleNakamaWatchRoomLeave, HandleNakamaWatchRoomSetControl, HandleNakamaWatchRoomForceTracks and HandleNakamaWatchRoomAutoSkip proceed even when the caller has no resolved identity (dataUserID==0 / username=='anon'), building a pool key of 'local:anon'. Practical impact is limited because the hub still validates the key against room.HostKey/Participants (ErrNotRoomHost / ErrParticipantUnknown), but the auth-gate pattern is inconsistent across the same handler file. Still present at HEAD (verified in current internal/handlers/nakama_rooms.go).

lowUnverified.title: Access-log Enricher re-resolves the session user from the bearer token, duplicating IdentityMiddleware's lookup on every request
lowUnverified.detail: f4ace33a's RequestUsername() re-extracts the bearer token and calls h.App.Database.GetSessionUser(token) directly because the lecho logging middleware (routes.go:58, global) runs before v1.Use(h.IdentityMiddleware) (routes.go:132, /v1-scoped) which performs the identical GetSessionUser(token) lookup moments later for any /v1 request. This is an extra synchronous DB round-trip per non-skipped request purely for log attribution; low-impact given in-process sqlite but a duplicate-work pattern on a hot path.

lowUnverified.title: streamManagers (per-user StreamManager map) and queuedDownloadFailures counter map have no eviction for departed users
lowUnverified.detail: 9b969cc3's Repository.streamManagers (keyed by userID, created lazily via smFor/GetOrSet) is never pruned when a user session ends/is evicted, so on a long-running networked server every distinct user who ever streamed keeps a permanent StreamManager (with its own preload cache) resident. Similarly c5a22f4e's queuedDownloadFailures counter map only clears on recovery or threshold-breach, not when a torrent item is removed via other paths (e.g. user-cancelled). Both are small, low-cardinality maps in practice (bounded by real user count / in-flight torrents) so impact is minor.
lowUnverified.evidence: internal/debrid/client/repository.go (9b969cc3, streamManagers field + smFor()); internal/debrid/client/download.go (c5a22f4e, queuedDownloadFailures)

lowUnverified.title: WatchRoomHub.Stop() (kills the 1s/500ms broadcast ticker goroutine) has no non-test caller
lowUnverified.detail: 5e8bc095 adds a process-lifetime ticker goroutine (runBroadcastLoop) started in NewWatchRoomHub, plus a Stop() method with sync.Once to end it. Manager.Stop()/Cleanup() (internal/nakama/nakama.go:425,456) never call watchRoomHub.Stop(); only watch_room_test.go references the hub without stopping it either. Currently harmless because NewWatchRoomHub is invoked exactly once per process (internal/nakama/nakama.go:252) and the goroutine dies with the process, but the shutdown path is effectively dead code / an unwired resource-lifecycle hook.

lowUnverified.title: Persisted DebridActiveStream row is only pruned lazily (TTL check at next process start), never on normal stream stop/close
lowUnverified.detail: 1719bcd8's persistActiveStream upserts a per-user 'last active stream' row on every stream start, but there is no call site that deletes/invalidates it when a stream is stopped or the player is closed — DeleteDebridActiveStream is only invoked inside loadPersistedActiveStream when the TTL has already expired at StreamManager construction time. Practically benign (single row per user, overwritten by the next start, and the client-side auto-resume in cbeb92c6 is separately gated on in-memory 'was active when ws dropped' state), but the DB record can outlive the user's session/intent by up to its 1-24h selection TTL with no explicit teardown path.

lowUnverified.title: Recurring nonsensical 'ponytail:' comment marker across unrelated files/commits
lowUnverified.detail: The token 'ponytail:' prefixes several caveat/aside comments across this batch (nakama-room-sync.ts SEEK_COOLDOWN_MS comment, cdngate.go x2, and earlier in stream.go per fable-audit quoting). It reads as an AI-generated placeholder/tag word with no domain meaning; harmless but an odd, unexplained convention that could confuse future readers grepping for real hazard markers (TODO/FIXME/HACK).

lowUnverified.title: Login brute-force throttle keeps an unbounded, never-evicted failures map keyed by attacker-supplied username
lowUnverified.detail: loginThrottle.failures/lockedAt are package-level maps keyed by the POSTed username; a failed attempt increments failures[username] but only lockedAt entries (>=5 failures) are ever deleted (and only on the next check after the 30s window). An attacker sweeping many distinct usernames with 1-4 failed attempts each grows the map forever with no TTL/cap/reaper. Low impact (small string+int entries) but genuinely unbounded and driven by external input.

lowUnverified.title: useScrollRestoration's scroll listener writes to sessionStorage on every scroll event with no debounce/throttle
lowUnverified.detail: 5d160f5d's new useScrollRestoration hook attaches a passive scroll listener that calls sessionStorage.setItem synchronously on every fired event (which can fire dozens of times per second during a scroll gesture), on the My List page. Impact is small (single small string write) but it is a hot-path write with no rAF/debounce guard, matching the 'missing debounce' perf pattern called out for this audit.

lowUnverified.title: fanOutBatchPreloads re-implements a chunk of preloadStreamWith's dedupe/store/persist logic inline instead of reusing it, and skips the negative-cache (preloadFailedAt) check that the main path has
lowUnverified.detail: 8b7148af's fanOutBatchPreloads manually duplicates the existing/inflight dedupe checks, eviction call, and preload-entry construction that preloadStreamWith already implements, rather than calling a shared helper — a second place that must be kept in sync if the storage/eviction contract changes (e.g. the F10 evictIfNeededLocked ordering fix in e24c94a2 landed only in the main path's hydrate call, not here). It also doesn't check/set preloadFailedAt, so a failing sibling-episode resolve isn't negative-cached the way a normal preload failure is.

lowUnverified.title: Denshi's CORS-injection for direct CDN playback is unconditional, not gated by the Direct CDN Playback setting
lowUnverified.detail: 508c9954 registers `session.defaultSession.webRequest.onHeadersReceived` once at startup and adds `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` to any response of resourceType `media` lacking one, regardless of whether the user has the "Direct CDN playback" toggle enabled. The commit message frames it as scoped to that feature ("Unblocks the Direct CDN playback toggle"), but the implementation applies to all media loads in the app at all times.

lowUnverified.title: Loading-screen artwork is fetched directly from the browser to a third-party host instead of through the server
lowUnverified.detail: 162c156e's `useAniZipArtwork` issues `fetch("https://api.ani.zip/mappings?anilist_id=...")` straight from each client (web/Denshi/Tenji), diverging from the pattern of the server fetching/caching ani.zip metadata for episode data server-side. Every distinct client independently re-hits api.ani.zip on every stream start (React Query cache is per-browser-session, `staleTime: Infinity` doesn't help across users/devices), and any CORS/availability change on api.ani.zip's side affects every client rather than one server-side call site.

lowUnverified.title: Commit 63311740 ('downgrade benign disconnect errors to trace') also bundles an unrelated player-thrashing fix with no mention in the message
lowUnverified.detail: The commit subject/body only describes isBenignStreamWriteErr log-level changes in httpstream.go, but the diff also adds the `openSignaled` field/gate to Manager and updateOpenStepLocked (manager.go, stream.go) — a distinct functional fix for the MpvCore open-thrashing regression from a1fb0208. Silent scope creep: reviewing the commit message alone would miss a real behavioral fix.

lowUnverified.title: Commit a1fb0208 also adds targetForClient() session-target downgrade logic, unrelated to and undisclosed by its stated subject
lowUnverified.detail: a1fb0208's message is entirely about preloaded-path step updates and phase timing diagnostics, but the diff also adds `Manager.targetForClient` (BeginOpen downgrades a session's MpvCore target to VideoCore for any client whose platform isn't 'denshi', since only Denshi can host mpv-prism) plus matching test coverage. This is a legitimate, seemingly-important fix (a plain web tab signaled via MpvCore would mount a player with no prism bridge) but is not mentioned anywhere in the commit message.

lowUnverified.title: CleanupWatchedPrewarms replaced an indexed SQL DELETE with an app-level full-table scan to support per-user refcounting
lowUnverified.detail: 800f59bd removes `DeleteDebridPrewarmsBelow` (a single `WHERE account_hash=? AND media_id=? AND episode_number<?` DELETE) and replaces it with `ListDebridPrewarms()` (loads every prewarm row) filtered/updated in Go, needed for the new per-user UserTags refcounting. Runs on every continue-watching prewarm tick (every 10 min) for every user with progress>1 on any candidate; likely fine at current row counts but is a real query-efficiency downgrade versus the prior indexed query.
